i32.description: 32-bit signed integer, the default integer type

i64.description: 64-bit signed integer for large whole numbers

i128.description: 128-bit signed integer for very large numbers

u8.description: 8-bit unsigned integer, range 0 to 255, used for bytes

usize.description: Pointer-sized unsigned integer for indexing and memory sizes

f64.description: 64-bit IEEE 754 double-precision floating-point, the default float

bool.description: Boolean type with values true and false

char.description: Unicode scalar value, 4 bytes representing a single Unicode character

str.description: String slice, a reference to a UTF-8 encoded string in memory

String.description: Growable UTF-8 encoded string type owned on the heap

Vec.description: Growable array type, the most common collection for owned sequences

Option.description: Represents an optional value: Some contains a value, None represents absence

Result.description: Type for operations that can fail: Ok for success, Err for failure with error info

Box.description: Heap-allocated smart pointer with single ownership, used for recursive types

HashMap.description: Hash-based key-value map with O(1) average lookup

Rc.description: Reference-counted pointer for shared ownership in single-threaded contexts

Arc.description: Atomic reference-counted pointer for safe shared ownership across threads
